response:
Nurse Emily organized a creative writing workshop for the 24 residents in her care facility, ensuring that each group of 6 participants would receive personalized attention. She planned to provide 3 writing prompts per group, which means she needed 3 prompts multiplied by the number of groups. Since there are 24 residents divided into groups of 6, there are 4 groups in total. Therefore, the initial number of writing prompts required is 3 prompts per group times 4 groups, which equals 12 prompts.

In addition to the initial prompts, Emily prepared 10 extra prompts to be shared equally among all the groups. These extra prompts will be distributed as needed, ensuring that all residents have ample material to work with. Emily also made sure that the writing prompts are stored in the library for easy access and that the library is well-organized to support the creative process of the residents.

The library serves as a central resource for all the writing materials, and Emily ensures that it is maintained and updated regularly. This way, the residents can benefit from a variety of writing prompts and other resources available in the library.

The total number of writing prompts Emily needs to prepare for the session, including both the initial and extra prompts, is 12 + 10 = 22.

The END.
